methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Asylon Robotics is a Series B stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $40M to date. The valuation universe consists of eight private peers in the sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation. No public comparables exist in this segment, limiting benchmark options.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach for this analysis. Using total capital raised of $40M as a baseline proxy for post-money valuation at Series A, a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B step-ups yields an implied valuation of $76M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while five same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap was found within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies), falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required to establish a reliable comparable median. Broadening to all-sector peers risked undue noise given the frontier-tech heterogeneity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $87M, centered on $76M. This represents a 1.6x to 2.2x multiple on total capital deployed. The primary uncertainty is the absence of tighter peer multiples; a wider comparable set at identical or near-identical stage and sector focus would strengthen conviction in the median. Additionally, company-specific factors—revenue, burn rate, and progress toward near-term milestones—remain outside the scope of this stage-based approach and warrant separate operational due diligence.
